subject: Math
domain: Number and Operations in Base Ten

lesson.story_mission: Join the Million Map team to decode city coordinates, compare giant supply totals, and round distances so the crew can plan a safe route.
lesson.mini_lesson: A digit gets its value from its place. Use commas to read thousands and millions, expand each nonzero digit by its value, compare from the greatest place, and round by checking the digit to the right.
lesson.objective: Read, write, compare, and round whole numbers from 0 to 1,000,000 using place value.
lesson.concept_explanation: In Grade 4, numbers can have ones, thousands, and millions periods. Zeros are important placeholders because they keep other digits in the correct place.
lesson.common_misconception: Learners may shift digits into the wrong place, skip zero placeholders, leave out a place in expanded form, or round to the wrong place.
lesson.real_world_connection: Place value helps people read population counts, compare distances, round budgets, and understand large numbers in news and science.
lesson.lesson_summary: Read each period, keep zero placeholders, compare from the left, and round using the digit just to the right of the target place.

audio.text: In this lesson, we use place value to understand numbers as large as one million. Listen for the places, watch zeros carefully, and use the models before you answer.
audio.label: Listen to Lesson

worked_examples.prompt: In 405,020, what digit is in the ten thousands place?
hints: Name the place or benchmark the question asks for.
Use commas and place-value periods to keep each digit in the right place.
Check zeros carefully because they hold places even when their value is zero.

feedback.correct: Yes! You used Grade 4 place-value reasoning.
feedback.incorrect: Try again by lining up the digits by place value.

worked_examples.explanation: The digit in the ten thousands place of 405,020 is 0.
worked_examples.read_aloud_text: In 405,020, what digit is in the ten thousands place?

worked_examples.explanation: 950,000 is between 900,000 and 1,000,000. The midpoint is 950,000, so it rounds to 1,000,000.
worked_examples.spoken_prompt: Round nine hundred fifty thousand to the nearest one hundred thousand.
worked_examples.read_aloud_text: Round nine hundred fifty thousand to the nearest one hundred thousand.

question_audio.text: Round nine hundred fifty thousand to the nearest one hundred thousand.
question_audio.label: Read Question

hint_ladder: First, read the commas as place-value periods: millions, thousands, and ones.
Next, underline the place the question asks about or the place you are rounding to.
Then, use the digit to the right when rounding, or compare digits from left to right when comparing numbers.
Finally, check that zeros are still holding their places in your answer.

misconception_bank.place_value_shift_error: The digit moved to a neighboring place. Line up the number in a place-value chart before answering.
misconception_bank.zero_placeholder_confusion: A zero can mean there are none of that place, but it still holds the place for other digits.
misconception_bank.expanded_form_error: Expanded form should add the value of each nonzero digit, not just list the digits.
misconception_bank.rounding_place_error: Round to the requested place only. Check the digit immediately to its right.

story.text: Welcome to Million Map Quest. You are helping the team read very large map numbers up to one million. Press Start Mission when you are ready to use place value like a math navigator. Watch for commas and zeros because they help every digit stay in the right place.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This page teaches the tools for the mission. Read the objective, vocabulary, concept, watch out, and real-world connection before you press Next. Use the place-value chart to see millions, hundred thousands, ten thousands, thousands, hundreds, tens, and ones.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: Watch this worked example before you answer on your own. The model lines up every digit by place, including zeros that hold empty places. Press Next after you can explain why the answer matches the requested place.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This guided demo is your supported try. Build it, read it, and check each place before independent practice. Press Next when the model and explanation make sense.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: Now try a practice question. Choose your answer or type it carefully, then use the hint if you need support. Read the question aloud and keep commas, zeros, and place names organized.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: This challenge asks you to apply place-value thinking with less help. Read the question, compare or round carefully, and check that your answer fits the requested place. Press Next after your answer is complete.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: This checkpoint shows what you can do independently. Answer carefully and use the visual model to verify your thinking. When you finish, you will earn your badge and save your growth.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: Great work on the Million Map mission. This badge celebrates your ability to read, write, compare, and round numbers up to one million. Press Save Growth Data to move to your profile.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: Your profile summarizes the mission and leads to more practice. If you want stronger mastery, choose Continue to Skill Practice and work through the focused levels. You can go back to the hub when you are ready.
profile.label: Read This Page
